params.text:
Types of records in care

You will meet many different documents, each with its own purpose. Recording in the right place, in the right way, keeps information easy to find.

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Match each record to what it is used for.

dialogs.answer: The person's central document: their needs, wishes, goals and how support should be delivered. Reviewed and updated as things change.
A running record of each visit or shift: care given, food and drink, mood, observations and anything needing follow-up.
Medication Administration Record — signed each time a medicine is given, refused or withheld, showing what, when and by whom.
A diagram used to record the exact location and description of skin damage, bruising, wounds or where a topical cream is applied.
Tracks what a person eats and drinks over the day, used where hydration or nutrition is a concern.
Key information passed between shifts or visits so the next worker knows what has happened and what to prioritise.
Completed after a fall, injury, medication error or other event — factual account of what happened and the action taken.
